metadata.title: Critical minerals / rare earths - the physical bottleneck under AI + defense, and the state-capitalism circularity
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (MP Materials/Apple/DoD press + SEC 8-K, CNBC, Critical Metals Corp/Tanbreez, TMC investor releases, China export-control reporting: Pillsbury, FDD, Fortune, CFR).
metadata.thesis: Rare earths/critical minerals are the physical chokepoint beneath BOTH the AI/electrification buildout (copper, NdFeB magnets) and the defense-contractor leg (REEs for missiles, radar, F-35, drones). The US response - government EQUITY + price floors + offtake - is itself a circular state-funding structure, and it has spawned a wave of speculative junior-miner equities riding the strategic narrative.

companies.name: MP Materials
facts: DoD bought $400M convertible preferred at $30.03 -> ~15% as-converted (Pentagon = LARGEST shareholder), Jul 2025
DoD $110/kg NdPr price FLOOR for 10 years (US pays the difference quarterly); DoD gets 30% of upside above $110 once 2nd magnet plant runs
$150M 12-yr govt loan for heavy-REE circuits (Dy/Tb/Sm) at Mountain Pass
Apple $500M recycled-magnet deal (shipments 2027), Fort Worth TX
companies.circular_structure: GOVERNMENT as circular funder: state takes equity + guarantees price + commits offtake -> de-risks supplier -> supplier serves defense+commercial. Structurally mirrors Nvidia/SoftBank funding OpenAI, but with the US Treasury/DoD as the recycling party.

companies.name: Critical Metals Corp
facts: Owns 92.5% of Tanbreez (Greenland), one of the world's largest REE deposits; European Lithium holds remaining 7.5%
$30M acceleration program (Mar 2026); first ore targeted Q4 2028 / 2029; concentrate export ~Q3 2029
Qualifies for up to $120M US EXIM financing; Trump admin reportedly discussed taking a stake
15-yr offtake with REalloys for 15% of concentrate
companies.circular_structure: Greenland geopolitics + govt-stake narrative; PRE-REVENUE - valuation rides the strategic story, classic junior-miner speculation.

companies.name: European Lithium
facts: Wolfsberg lithium project (Austria); spun CRML via SPAC; retains 7.5% of Tanbreez

companies.name: The Metals Company
facts: Deep-sea polymetallic nodules (Ni/Cu/Co/Mn); CEO Gerard Barron at White House for Trump's Apr 2025 EO 'Unleashing America's Offshore Critical Minerals'
Filed DSHMRA seabed permits within a week of the EO; EO directs DoD/DOE to assess National Defense Stockpile offtake + DPA
Partner PAMCO smelted a 2,000-t nodule sample (Feb 2025)
companies.circular_structure: Pumped on executive-order optionality + DoD-stockpile narrative; pre-commercial and environmentally litigated.

companies.name: Ucore Rare Metals
facts: RapidSX REE separation tech; Louisiana Strategic Metals Complex; has received US/Canada DoD-linked awards (lower-confidence specifics)
companies.circular_structure: Processing-independence narrative; relies on govt grants/offtake.

annual_volumes_defense_vs_ai.summary: Quantify the annual physical tonnage and split demand between DEFENSE (per-platform, low-tonnage, high-criticality) and AI/electrification (bulk magnets/copper, high-tonnage) - #48. Mine/processing shares are fact; the defense-vs-AI demand split is an estimate (contested).

rare_earths.world_mine_2025: ~390,000 t REO mined (USGS order of magnitude); China ~70% of mine output and ~90% of separation/magnet processing - the binding constraint is PROCESSING, not ore.
rare_earths.magnet_rees: NdFeB demand is dominated by Nd/Pr (light REE) plus small Dy/Tb (heavy REE) additions for high-temp coercivity; heavy-REE separation is ~100% China - the exact step weaponized Apr-2025.

antimony.world_mine_2023: ~83,000 t mined antimony (USGS); China ~48% of mine output, plus Tajikistan/Russia/Myanmar - and China ~90% of REFINED/processed antimony. The US had NO domestic mined antimony before Perpetua/Stibnite.
antimony.price_path: China's Dec-2024 export ban drove antimony from ~$25k/t to ~$60k/t peak; ~$51.8k/t (Jun 2026) after the Nov-9-2025 suspension - still ~2-3x pre-ban. Military-end-user ban + licensing retained.

defense_vs_ai_split.defense: LOW tonnage, HIGH criticality and no substitute: REE magnets in missile fin actuators, radar, F-35 (Adamas ~23kg SmCo/NdFeB per airframe; the '920 lb per F-35' figure is CONTESTED - it conflates whole-program magnet mass), Virginia/Columbia subs, drones; antimony in ammunition primers, armor-piercing rounds, night-vision/IR. A single platform needs kilograms, but a supply cut halts production entirely.
defense_vs_ai_split.ai_electrification: HIGH tonnage: NdFeB magnets in datacenter cooling/HVAC motors, grid build-out, EV traction motors and wind turbines; plus copper for power/interconnect (cross-ref macro-futures-vs-physical copper). AI/electrification is the VOLUME driver; defense is the CRITICALITY driver. Both draw on the same China-controlled separation step.
defense_vs_ai_split.grade: contested (directional split; per-platform figures vary by source)

china_squeeze.fact: China controls ~70% of REE mining and ~90% of processing. In 2025 it weaponized this: Apr 4 2025 export controls on 7 heavy REEs (Dy, Gd, Lu, Sm, Sc, Tb, Y) + magnets; Oct 2025 added 5 more (Er, Eu, Ho, Tm, Yb); plus Ga/Ge/Sb/W/In/Bi/Te/Mo controls hitting semiconductors and defense. The Nov 7 2025 Trump-Xi deal PAUSED most controls for 1 year BUT China RETAINED the Apr-2025 case-by-case licensing system that chokes Western military supply.

linkage: Critical minerals connect the original five-sector bubble framing: semis + AI (copper/power/magnets) and DEFENSE contractors (REE for weapons) share the same Chinese-controlled chokepoint. The US policy response introduces a SECOND circular structure parallel to the AI loop - the STATE as vendor-financier (MP Materials) - while seeding a speculative junior-miner sub-bubble (CRML/TMC/Ucore) priced on government-deal narratives rather than production. Honest grade: MP Materials = real; the juniors = strategic-story speculation.

edges.raw_instrument: auto-deny foreign-military licensing on six separated REEs (~90% of processing)
edges.notes: the binding chokepoint is SEPARATION, retained under case-by-case licensing

edges.raw_instrument: export licensing + military-end-user ban (~90% of refined antimony)
edges.notes: Dec-2024 ban; suspended Nov-2025 but licensing + mil-end-user ban retained

edges.raw_instrument: NdFeB magnets for missiles / F-35 / radar / drones (military-critical, no substitute)
ammunition primers / armor-hardening / night-vision IR optics (military-critical)
edges.notes: no domestic mined US source pre-Stibnite
